5.reason: [The function 'gets' is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library since C11; using it may cause compilation errors or warnings with modern compilers. It should be replaced with 'fgets'.]

7.reason: Loop processes up to the end; when i is the last index, accessing c[i+1] is '\0' and dash at end will not be output.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Loop processes up to the end; when i is the last index, accessing c[i+1] is '\0' and dash at end will not be output.

12.reason: Does not check i>0, causing out-of-bounds when i=0; also does not ensure both characters belong to the same character type (lowercase, uppercase, digit) as required.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Accesses c[i-1] without checking i>0; causes undefined behavior when i=0. Also condition alone does not ensure same type.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  Does not check i>0, causing out-of-bounds when i=0; also does not ensure both characters belong to the same character type (lowercase, uppercase, digit) as required.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Logical/Runtime Error] Accessing c[i-1] when i=0 causes out-of-bounds memory access. Additionally, if expansion conditions fail, the '-' character is not copied to the output array 'a', violating the requirement to output the string unchanged.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Accessing c[i-1] when i=0 causes out-of-bounds memory access; also fails to verify c[i+1] is not '\0'.

16.reason: [Incorrect condition: it checks if the left char is >= '0' and right char <= '9', but does not ensure both are digits. Should check both c[i-1] and c[i+1] are between '0' and '9'.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Only checks left >= '0' and right <= '9'; should verify both are digits. Missing else for dash when no expansion occurs.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  Insufficient: only checks lower bound of first and upper bound of second; does not verify both are digits (e.g., c[i-1]='0', c[i+1]=':' would pass incorrectly).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Logical Error] This condition does not strictly verify that both characters are digits. It should explicitly check the range for both c[i-1] and c[i+1] (e.g., c[i-1]>='0' && c[i-1]<='9' && c[i+1]>='0' && c[i+1]<='9').
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Incorrect range validation; only checks c[i-1]>='0' and c[i+1]<='9', allowing mixed types like 'a-9'. Should verify both characters are digits.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrect condition: it checks if the left char is >= '0' and right char <= '9', but does not ensure both are digits. Should check both c[i-1] and c[i+1] are between '0' and '9'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrect condition: should check that BOTH c[i-1] and c[i+1] are digits, but this only checks c[i-1] >= '0' and c[i+1] <= '9', allowing invalid mixes like 'a'-'9'. Also, no check that c[i-1] <= '9' or c[i+1] >= '0'.]
